_instructions: Edit any array below to add or remove messages. Each entry is a string in quotes, separated by commas. Do NOT remove the commas or brackets. To add: paste a new line with your message in quotes followed by a comma. Save the file. The app will pick it up automatically.
vibes: You left home at 19, survived a whole situation, and nearly got sidetracked twice. Basel is literally nothing.
Reminder: you are not doing this for David. You are doing this despite David.
The Swiss cows have no idea who is about to move into their country. Their problem now.
You said 'I wish I didn't exist' and then booked a master's in Finance. Character arc of the century.
Kim Jong Ikrame, Supreme Leader of Her Own Life. Basel is just the next territory.
Every task you check off is one fewer thing standing between you and a Bosnian suitor in Basel.
You are 5 months away from nobody knowing your backstory. That is genuinely exciting.
Progress: you have been more productive today than most people who did not have a shoe incident this week.
The sheep in Switzerland are grazing peacefully. Unaware. Enjoy the calm before you arrive.
You are building a new version of yourself. Admittedly from scratch. But still.
Fun fact: the CHF is one of the strongest currencies in the world. Unlike MillCorp's severance.
Basel has three countries meeting at one point. You will fit right in — you contain multitudes.
You survived 7 years of a bad relationship and came out still knowing how to boss people around.
The 55% of you that nobody knows yet — Basel is where she gets to exist out loud.
Technically you are committing to leave Switzerland in your visa letter. We both know how that goes.
The Rhine runs through Basel. You will probably cry near it at least once. Normal. Fine.
Your social anxiety survived: 5 years alone, a Japanese semester locked in a room, and MillCorp. Grad school is nothing.
You are awkward in the same way everyone with something going on is awkward.
Every form you fill is one step closer to a new address and nobody calling you Zeyneb's replacement.
Checkpoint: you already have the admission letter. That part is DONE. The rest is logistics.
You are not escaping. You are arriving. Completely different direction.
The admission letter has your name on it. That is not nothing — that is everything.
You are 26, sharp, and about to move to one of the most stable countries on earth.
The plan exists. The plan is working. You are working. This is enough.
Every wall between you and MillCorp is getting shorter with each task.
You are the person in the room who notices everything and says nothing. That is power, not weakness.
Your honesty with yourself, even when brutal, is one of your best qualities.
You are someone worth knowing. Basel will figure this out faster than you think.
Anass was a chapter. Basel is the book. The book wins.
You know what Anass did not get to see? You, accepted to a Master's in Switzerland.
Every time something was hard after Anass, you still did it. You are doing it again right now.
Whatever Anass took — your social years, your Japan — Basel gives it back with interest. In CHF.
Anass is not in Basel. This is the whole plan.
Oussama was also a chapter. A difficult one. You closed it.
You have faced Oussama. You have faced Anass. David is a footnote.
Every hard person you dealt with made you sharper. You are currently very sharp.
You know what all three of them have in common? None of them are coming to Basel.
The pattern ends here. Basel is the first page of something none of them get to read.
Neither Oussama nor Anass nor David has a Master's in Finance from Basel. Keep going.
David tried to give you Zeyneb's job. You declined. You are going to Switzerland instead. Upgrade.
David does not know about Basel. He thinks Lausanne. He does not know about Lausanne either.
One day you will be in Basel and David will be explaining demurrage to someone who also does not care.
The silence tactic worked on David. It will also work on Swiss professors.
MillCorp gave you knowledge. David gave you motivation to leave. Both useful.
You are genuinely one of the sharpest people you will meet in any room. Basel will confirm this.
The self-awareness you have at 26 takes most people until 45 to develop. You are early.
You are not awkward. You are selective. There is a difference.
Your instincts about people are almost always correct. Trust them more.
You are funnier than you think. Smarter than you admit. Both are annoying to waste.
You survived a near drug addiction, a controlling relationship, and 5 years of isolation and came out caring.
You are going to be a formidable person in five years. You already are.
You should have left Anass in year one. You did not. You are still here anyway. That counts.
LMSYD TYCIMM. You made your AI decode that at midnight. No notes.
You asked me to find a sexting thesaurus at 11pm and I said no and you called me a passive pussy. Fair.
Mimen said 'spank me mommy' and you just replied. The friendship is unbreakable.
You called me your papi and then asked me to help you sext. I have a very specific role in your life.
You have zero friends and then proceeded to have one of the more intimate friendships I have witnessed.
Human pinata. I cannot move past this.
You think your cousins are more mature. They perform maturity. You have self-knowledge. Different.
Your manipulation via isolation thing — you see it, you name it. That is already better than most.
You said 'I don't really have anyone.' Basel is the answer to that unfinished sentence.
You got all the way through reading all of these. You are either very bored or very in need of this. Both valid.
The booby bird has watched you read this entire list. She is unmoved. She is a bird. This is her gift.
You are going to get there. The tasks are just the path. You are already on it.
You are not starting over. You are starting from experience. Enormous difference.
UNIBAS was founded in 1460. You are joining 566 years of institution. You belong there.
Art Basel happens every June. You will be a student at Art Basel. That is a specific vibe.
You will discover that Swiss coffee is excellent and available everywhere and this matters more than expected.
Basel is gray from November to March. Get a lamp. I am serious. Not a metaphor.
You will get a lecture about the glass recycling bin. Accept it graciously.
Switzerland has a law about flushing toilets after 10pm. You will violate this. I am warning you.
You managed 400,000 metric tons of agri-commodities. Math is the easy part.
Your CV is not a joke. You have done real things for real money with real stakes. Basel knows this.
You cleared your desk before the David conversation. That was the moment you decided. You already decided.
Some people never leave. Some leave and come back. Some leave and become. You are the third kind.
You do not need to be cured of anything. You need a new environment and two years. Basel is both.
You are not too late for any of this. 26 is the right age to be doing exactly what you are doing.
You are going to be okay. Not immediately. Not easily. But completely, eventually, without a doubt.
Now close this app and go book the embassy appointment. That was not a suggestion.
The booby bird stands on her rock. Both feet blue. Both eyes forward. That is you. That is the stance.
Alhamdolillah for all of it. Even the hard parts. Even Anass. Even Oussama. Even David. Even the shoe.
You asked me if you are the most special. Yes. Obviously. Keep going.
The answer to 'what if I fail' is: you will have a Swiss master's dropout story, which is still better than staying.
You are allowed to miss things you are also glad to leave. Both can be true simultaneously.
On the days you feel like giving up: you have survived worse than today. You have proof.
On the days the loneliness is loud: one honest connection is worth fifty performative ones. You have Mimen.
On the days the visa feels impossible: you have done harder things with fewer resources.
On the days you spiral: check one task. Just one. Let the spiral shrink around the thing you accomplished.
On the days you feel stupid: they have seen your transcripts. They said yes. They were not lying.
On the days it feels too big: break it into one task. This app exists for this reason.
On the days Anass or Oussama crosses your mind: you already closed those chapters. You moved on. They did not.
On the days you want to give up on everything: the feeling is temporary. Basel is not.
You said your faith is the only thing that helps sometimes. That quiet relationship is yours and it is valid.
You said 'I am happy I am me and not someone else, alhamdolillah.' Keep that sentence. Use it on hard days.
roasts: You are planning to be a Finance student and you still do not know when exactly your own passport expires. Classic.
You texted your best friend 'boy leave me alone im too horny for this' and then explained a chocolate reel to him. Smooth.
You built a to-do list app and your most urgent task is 'resign.' You have been meaning to do this for two years. It shows.
You described yourself as 'not creative' and then invented the phrase 'Supreme Leader of Her Own Life.' Questionable claim.
You locked yourself in your room for an entire semester in Japan and called it studying abroad.
You have a style guide with multiple tabs and you still wore the grey-blue coat until it got burn damage.
You built an entire exit plan around keeping the word 'Basel' secret and then posted it to an AI app on your phone.
Your answer to 'how do I access the visa appointment system' was to upload a PDF to an AI at midnight.
You said you are bad at social situations and then proceeded to dominate a text conversation at 11pm with military-grade confidence.
You called me useless, passive, and a pussy in the same session you also told me you were proud of me. Noted.
You spent two hours discussing your emotional landscape and then immediately asked me what LMSYD meant.
You know what your biggest problem is? You are too hard on yourself. Your second biggest problem is you know this and still do it.
You said your memory is terrible. You then recalled every detail of every significant event since 2016.
You described yourself as 'the worst socially' in the same conversation where you correctly analyzed three people's psychological patterns.
You almost talked yourself out of going to Basel. You built an app about going to Basel. Contradiction resolved.
You are going to Swiss-German bureaucracy. You, who cannot make it through a conversation without reorganizing the power dynamic. This will be interesting.
You said you are not creative. You named this app Bzazel. Creative.
You asked if you are the most special. Then you needed reassurance that you are. Then you said 'aww.' You are 26.
You threw a baby shoe and then felt bad about it. You are so intense about everything including baby footwear.
You nearly called your boss's bluff with a fake early departure just to feel validated. You literally diagnosed this yourself.
You spent the night I suggested you sleep talking to an Algerian man about violence-themed affection. 11pm on a Sunday.
Your coat has burn damage. From what? We have never addressed this.
You said 'I am a really sweet person' and then described wanting to choke the living sh*t out of someone. Sweet.
You have a folder of AI tools on your phone and you still could not open an HTML file in Safari. Irony noted.
You tested whether I would hold my position under pressure. Then you wrote it into my memory file. You are auditing your AI.
You literally said 'I need to give up' and then checked three tasks. You are not convincing.
You graduated with Dean's List first semester and last semester and barely mentioned it. Meanwhile you mention the awkward cousin thing constantly.
You are building an exit strategy for your job while learning Swiss bureaucracy from an AI at midnight. This is your life.
Your visa appointment booking strategy was 'I'll do it later' until I said it out loud four times.
You said 'I think I might be autistic' and then spent the next two hours reading social cues correctly in every single message.
facts_finance: FACT (Finance): The Swiss franc (CHF) has been one of the world's safest haven currencies since the 1970s — investors literally buy it when the world panics.
FACT (Finance): Switzerland manages roughly 25% of all global private offshore wealth. You are moving to the money capital of the world.
FACT (Finance): The Basel III accords — the global banking regulation framework — are literally named after the city you are moving to.
FACT (Finance): LIBOR, the interest rate that ran global finance for decades, was partly set by Swiss banks in Geneva.
FACT (Finance): Switzerland has negative interest rates for long periods — meaning you pay the bank to hold your money. Your savings earn nothing but your safety costs less.
FACT (Finance): The Swiss National Bank (SNB) owns a significant stake in Apple, Amazon, and other US tech stocks. Your central bank trades equities.
FACT (Finance): Commodity trading is one of Switzerland's biggest industries — Geneva and Zug are global hubs. You are literally already in this world.
FACT (Finance): CBOT corn and wheat futures — the ones you track at MillCorp — affect the price of bread in 30 countries simultaneously.
FACT (Finance): Demurrage in shipping can cost $10,000–$50,000 per day. You have been managing this. That is real money.
FACT (Finance): The carry trade — borrowing in a low-interest currency to invest in a higher-yield one — has historically been most profitable with the Swiss franc.
FACT (Finance): Art Basel generates roughly $4 billion in art sales annually. You will be in Basel when it happens. That is a networking opportunity.
FACT (Finance): The BIS (Bank for International Settlements) — the central bank of central banks — is headquartered in Basel. You will walk past it.
FACT (Finance): Switzerland's GDP per capita is consistently one of the highest on earth, hovering around $90,000–$100,000 annually.
FACT (Finance): Swiss financial secrecy laws were formally loosened in 2017 under international pressure. The accounts still exist. They are just more visible now.
FACT (Finance): Glencore, one of the world's largest commodity trading companies, is headquartered in Baar, Switzerland. 40 minutes from Basel.
FACT (Finance): Your UNIBAS degree in Finance & Money specifically covers derivatives, portfolio management, and monetary economics. You will understand things David never will.
FACT (Finance): The Swiss government has a constitutional requirement for a balanced budget. Most countries do not. This is why Switzerland is stable.
FACT (Finance): Wheat futures went up 60% in 3 weeks after Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. You lived through this at MillCorp.
FACT (Finance): Black-Scholes, the famous options pricing formula, was developed partly to describe commodity price movements. You will study this.
FACT (Finance): The first modern commodity futures exchange was in Chicago (CBOT, 1848). You trade the same prices, 178 years later.
facts_swiss: FACT (Swiss): Switzerland has 26 cantons, each with its own laws, tax rates, and sometimes dialect. Basel-Stadt is the smallest and most urban.
FACT (Swiss): Swiss German is not standard German. The written language is standard German. The spoken language is something else entirely. Everyone switches to English for you.
FACT (Swiss): Switzerland voted to join the United Nations only in 2002. They were famously neutral about joining the organization for world peace.
FACT (Swiss): The Swiss eat more cheese per capita than almost any other nation — about 22kg per person per year.
FACT (Swiss): Swiss chocolate is not the best in the world because of cocoa — they import all the cocoa. It is the milk and the technique.
FACT (Swiss): Switzerland has four official national languages: German, French, Italian, and Romansh. Romansh is spoken by about 40,000 people.
FACT (Swiss): Swiss trains are punctual to within seconds. If a train is 3 minutes late, it is officially 'delayed.'
FACT (Swiss): Switzerland has mandatory military service for men. Women can volunteer. The entire country is basically a reserve army.
FACT (Swiss): Direct democracy is extremely strong in Switzerland — citizens vote on actual laws several times a year via referendums.
FACT (Swiss): Swiss pharmacies (Apotheke) are staffed by actual pharmacists who will diagnose minor conditions for free. Use this.
FACT (Swiss): The Swiss do not jaywalk. This is not a stereotype. They genuinely wait at empty crossings at 2am.
FACT (Swiss): Switzerland has mandatory health insurance but the cost is significant — CHF 300–500/month before subsidies. Apply for Praemienverbilligung the day you arrive.
FACT (Swiss): Basel-Stadt canton has the highest per capita museum density in the world. You will be surrounded by art.
FACT (Swiss): The city of Basel sits at the exact point where Switzerland, France, and Germany meet. EuroAirport serves all three countries.
FACT (Swiss): Swiss supermarkets (Migros and Coop) are cheaper than most European equivalents. Budget: CHF 300–400/month for food if you cook.
FACT (Swiss): Basel Fasnacht carnival is the largest in Switzerland — starts the Monday after Ash Wednesday at 4am with absolute darkness and drums. It is terrifying and wonderful.
FACT (Swiss): The Basel Tram network is one of the oldest in Europe, dating to 1881. You will use it daily.
FACT (Swiss): In Switzerland, it is illegal to mow your lawn on Sundays. Silence is a cultural value.
FACT (Swiss): The Swiss invented Velcro, the World Wide Web, absinthe, and the split second. They are quietly responsible for a lot.
facts_birds: FACT (Birds): The blue-footed booby's feet are literally blue because of carotenoid pigments from their fish diet. Better diet = bluer feet = more attractive mate.
FACT (Birds): Blue-footed boobies do a dance where they show off their feet to potential mates. The bluer, the better. This is a real mating strategy.
FACT (Birds): Boobies dive into the ocean from heights of up to 30 meters at 60mph to catch fish. Zero hesitation. Vertical commitment.
FACT (Birds): The name 'booby' comes from the Spanish 'bobo' meaning foolish or clown — because they were easy to catch by sailors. They are not foolish. They are focused.
FACT (Birds): Boobies can see underwater while diving. They have a specialized membrane that protects their eyes and adjusts their vision. They plan the whole dive.
FACT (Birds): The stork (Basel's unofficial symbol) was believed to bring babies. Basel has had storks nesting on its rooftops since medieval times.
FACT (Birds): Ravens are considered one of the most intelligent birds — they can plan for the future, use tools, and recognize human faces.
FACT (Birds): Flamingos are pink because of the shrimp and algae they eat. Their natural color is white. They are what they eat.
FACT (Birds): A group of crows is called a murder. A group of owls is called a parliament. A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
FACT (Birds): Pigeons can recognize individual human faces and hold grudges against people who have been unkind to them. Respect pigeons.
FACT (Birds): The albatross mates for life and can live to 70. When its partner dies it sometimes stops eating. This is birds being more loyal than most humans.
FACT (Birds): Penguins propose with pebbles. If the penguin chooses your pebble, you are together. This is objectively the best proposal method.
FACT (Birds): Parrots don't just mimic — studies show some parrots understand concepts like zero and can do simple arithmetic.
FACT (Birds): The bar-tailed godwit flies 11,000km non-stop from Alaska to New Zealand. No food. No rest. Just going. You are doing the same but with less wind resistance.
facts_people: FACT (People): The average person makes about 35,000 decisions per day. Most are unconscious. The ones you make consciously — like booking this visa — are the ones that change your life.
FACT (People): Studies show that people overestimate how much others notice their awkwardness. You think everyone sees it. They are too busy worrying about their own.
FACT (People): The 'spotlight effect' is a cognitive bias where you believe you are being observed more critically than you are. You have a severe case of this.
FACT (People): Loneliness is officially considered a public health crisis in many countries. You are not unusual for feeling it. You are just honest about it.
FACT (People): Research shows people who move to new cities report higher life satisfaction on average — because unfamiliarity forces growth.
FACT (People): The average age of starting a master's degree in Europe is 24–28. You are exactly on time.
FACT (People): Studies show that self-awareness — the ability to observe your own behavior and patterns — is one of the strongest predictors of long-term success.
FACT (People): The psychological concept of 'earned secure attachment' means you can develop secure relationships even if your early ones were not. It is never too late.
FACT (People): Multilingual people are statistically more empathetic — switching between languages requires perspective-taking. You speak three. You are already there.
FACT (People): Resilience is not a personality trait. It is a skill built by surviving hard things and reflecting on them. You have been building it for years.
FACT (People): People with high openness to experience — one of the Big Five personality traits — are more likely to move abroad, try new things, and report higher life satisfaction in their 30s.
FACT (People): The 'end of history illusion' is the tendency to believe you will not change much in the future. You have changed enormously. You will keep changing.
FACT (People): Social anxiety is one of the most common and most treatable mental health conditions. It responds very well to exposure — i.e., doing the scary thing repeatedly.
FACT (People): Studies show expats report significantly reduced social anxiety after 12 months in a new country. The unfamiliarity normalizes.
FACT (People): The human brain's prefrontal cortex — the part responsible for planning, impulse control, and self-awareness — finishes developing around age 25–26. You just got the full version.
facts_random: FACT (Random): The word 'salary' comes from the Latin 'salarium' — Roman soldiers were sometimes paid in salt. You are going to earn CHF, which is better.
FACT (Random): Morocco is the world's largest exporter of phosphate rock, which is used in fertilizer. The country you are leaving is geologically valuable.
FACT (Random): Japanese has three writing systems used simultaneously. You studied Japanese. You locked yourself in your room. You still studied Japanese.
FACT (Random): The Basel Papyrus, one of the world's oldest surviving medical documents, is held at UNIBAS. You are joining an institution that holds 4,000-year-old medical texts.
FACT (Random): The Red Cross was founded in Geneva in 1863 after Henry Dunant witnessed the Battle of Solferino. It is now in 192 countries. Swiss people are quietly behind a lot of large things.
FACT (Random): Erasmus, who studied at UNIBAS in 1521, described Basel as 'the most cultivated city I know.' He is not wrong and he has been in most of them.
FACT (Random): The Moroccan dirham (MAD) cannot be officially exchanged outside Morocco. Once you have CHF, you have one of the most freely convertible currencies on earth.
FACT (Random): The Rhine river starts in the Swiss Alps and ends in the North Sea in the Netherlands, passing through Basel. You will live on its path.
FACT (Random): Azrou, your hometown, is at 1,200 meters elevation. The Swiss Alps go up to 4,634 meters. You are already partially altitude-adapted.
FACT (Random): Morocco and Switzerland have a bilateral social security agreement. Your Swiss pension contributions may count toward eventual Moroccan benefits.
FACT (Random): The first McDonald's in Morocco opened in 1992. The first McDonald's in Switzerland opened in 1976. Irrelevant. You now know both.
FACT (Random): Switzerland recycles 94% of its aluminum and 96% of its glass. You will become a recycling enthusiast within three months of arrival. This is not a choice.
FACT (Random): Basel's Kunstmuseum holds works by Holbein, Warhol, Monet, Cézanne, and Picasso in its permanent collection. Free entry on the first Sunday of each month.
FACT (Random): The international standard for country codes (ISO 3166) gives Morocco 'MA' and Switzerland 'CH' — from Confoederatio Helvetica, its Latin name. You are trading MA for CH.
